items.title: From confusion matrix counts to P/R/F1
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  cm["Confusion matrix at threshold t"] --> tp["TP, FP, TN, FN"]
  tp --> prec["Precision = TP / (TP + FP)"]
  tp --> rec["Recall = TP / (TP + FN)"]
  prec --> f1["F1 = 2PR / (P + R)"]
  rec --> f1
items.caption: All three metrics require a fixed threshold and labeled evaluation set. Count definitions live in the Confusion Matrix topic.

items.title: Threshold shift and the precision–recall trade-off
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  lowT["Lower threshold"] --> morePos["More predicted positives"]
  morePos --> highRec["Recall tends up"]
  morePos --> lowPrec["Precision tends down"]
  highT["Higher threshold"] --> fewerPos["Fewer predicted positives"]
  fewerPos --> lowRec["Recall tends down"]
  fewerPos --> highPrec["Precision tends up"]
items.caption: Moving the cutoff changes operating point along a precision–recall curve; no single threshold optimizes both simultaneously in general.
